pr.title: ft/ftKirby: decompile Kirby special N and Hi moves
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled several Kirby special-move routines in ftKb_SpecialN.c and ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, primarily Final Cutter startup, Inhale swallow-distance checks, and Pikachu-copy Skull Bash collision/bounce handling. The PR replaced placeholder comments with C implementations, added one missing ftCommon include and an extern collision box declaration, and used MWCC dont_inline pragmas to preserve matching around the Inhale helper/callers. Reported matching impact included two new 100% matches for ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter plus near-complete improvements for several remaining functions.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter. Both reset Kirby Final Cutter command and move variables, call Fighter_ChangeMotionState for ground or air Final Cutter startup, start animation processing with ftAnim_8006EBA4, spawn effect 0x494, set x2219_b0, and install efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 as an item Inhale swallow proximity check. It computes Kirby mouth-relative position from cur_pos, specialn_x_offset_inhaled, specialn_y_offset_inhaled, and facing_dir; compares item distance from it_802F23AC against squared specialn_inhale_velocity; calls it_802F2810; then branches to air or ground follow-up via ftKb_SpecialN_800F63EC or ftKb_SpecialN_800F6388.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8 as the analogous fighter-victim Inhale swallow proximity check using ftCo_800BD19C and ftCo_800BD620 instead of item helpers.
Added #pragma dont_inline around ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 and around capture animation callers to prevent MWCC from inlining 800F5DE8 into callers, according to the PR summary.
Added include for ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.h in ftKb_SpecialN.c.

Implemented ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll for Kirby's Pikachu-copy Skull Bash transition from air start to grounded states, preserving current animation frame and restoring callbacks such as death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, deal_dmg_cb, and x21F8.

Implemented ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll for Pikachu-copy Skull Bash ground collision/wall interaction, using ft_800824A0 with ftKb_Init_803CB758, changing to ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNEndR, restoring callbacks, copying self_vel.x to gr_vel, and zeroing vertical/Z velocity.

Implemented fn_80105978 as a wall-bounce/direction reversal helper that rotates the model with ftPartSetRotY and negates self velocity, ground velocity, ground acceleration, several mv.pr.specialn fields, facing_dir, and x34 vector components.
Declared extern ftCollisionBox ftKb_Init_803CB758 in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c.
Made minor formatting-only line wrapping changes around Luigi Fire item/effect spawning in fn_800F98F4.
smart_moves: Used shared structure between ground and aerial Final Cutter enter functions while preserving distinct motion state IDs: ftKb_MS_SpecialHi1 versus ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi1.

Computed the Inhale target point once from dat_attrs offsets and facing_dir, then compared squared distance to squared velocity/threshold, avoiding an unnecessary square root and matching the apparent original behavior.
Separated item and fighter Inhale capture paths cleanly: item path uses target_item_gobj with it_802F23AC/it_802F2810, fighter path uses victim_gobj with ftCo_800BD19C/ftCo_800BD620.

Reinstalled important callbacks after motion-state transitions in Pikachu-copy collision handlers, a recurring Melee fighter-decomp pattern needed to preserve behavior after Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Used #pragma dont_inline as a targeted matching tactic rather than contorting source logic, explicitly documenting that MWCC inlining of ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 had to be suppressed.
Implemented wall-bounce reversal comprehensively by negating not only velocities but also move-specific direction and acceleration fields, which is important for copied-move state consistency.
decomp_lessons: For fighter special enter routines, reset cmd_vars and move-state scratch fields before Fighter_ChangeMotionState, then re-fetch the Fighter pointer after animation/effect setup if the original code pattern does so.
Effect spawning during special-move startup may need hitlag pause/resume callbacks installed immediately afterward so spawned effects freeze consistently with the fighter.
Inhale/capture logic can use dat_attrs offsets plus facing_dir to build a mouth-relative target position; keep item and fighter victim paths separate when they call different engine subsystems.
When a helper must remain a callable function for matching, MWCC dont_inline pragmas may be preferable to changing the helper body or its callers.
Motion-state collision transitions often require callback restoration after Fighter_ChangeMotionState; missing this can be a semantic bug even if the transition itself matches.

For copied specials such as Kirby with Pikachu copy, collision code may live in Kirby-specific files but follow the copied fighter's movement concepts, including charge dash, landing transitions, and wall-bounce direction reversal.
naming_conventions: Kirby special-N functions use the ftKb_SpecialN_ address-suffix style when behavior names are not yet recovered, e.g. ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 and ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8.
Kirby copied Pikachu special-N routines use the Pr prefix in names such as ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll and ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll.

Ground and aerial variants are distinguished with Air in the function and motion-state names, e.g. ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter versus ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter and ftKb_MS_SpecialHi1 versus ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi1.
Collision handlers use the _Coll suffix; animation handlers use the _Anim suffix; enter routines use _Enter.
Unidentified local/static helpers may retain fn_ address-style names, as with fn_80105978.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added #pragma dont_inline on/off around ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 to prevent MWCC from inlining it into capture animation callers.
Kept a local scoped block around distance calculation/comparison in the Inhale helpers, which may help reproduce register lifetime or stack behavior.

Used a f64 dist in ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 for the item-distance path while ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8 uses f32 dist for the fighter-distance path, reflecting type-sensitive matching needs shown in the diff.
Preserved exact motion-state flags such as 0x0C4C5092U in copied-special collision transitions.
Used explicit zeroing patterns and field assignments for cmd_vars, move fields, self_vel, and gr_vel rather than abstracting them, keeping source close to expected codegen.
Automated report showed 100.00% fuzzy matches for ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter, while several other functions were near matches rather than perfect according to the issue comment.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The automated match report credited two new 100% matches: ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter.
The automated report listed near-complete but not perfect improvements for ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8, ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll, fn_80105978, ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8.

The PR summary states fuzzy_match_percent was 100.0 for all functions, but the included automated report gives lower percentages for some unmatched items; treat exact per-function match status as mixed evidence from the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that improve ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 from the reported 91.96% unmatched-item score to a full match.
Search for other uses of #pragma dont_inline in fighter decomp files to compare MWCC inlining workarounds.
Inspect related Kirby copied Pikachu special-N functions around ftKb_PrSpecialAirNLoop_Coll, ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Coll, and ftKb_PrSpecialNHit_Coll for remaining placeholders.
Search for other fighter special enter routines that spawn effects and install efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks.
Compare ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll and fn_80105978 against Pikachu's original Skull Bash collision code for naming or struct-field recovery opportunities.
